originalTitle: ‘Truly junk’: E-waste from rich nations floods local markets in Nigeria
title: Uh Oh! Lots of Old Gadgets in Our Markets!
content:
Hello, amazing friends! It's me, Kosi, your friendly news helper from beautiful Africa! Today, we're going to talk about something super important for our home, Nigeria. Have you ever seen lots of old, broken phones, TVs, or computers in the market? Sometimes they look like they've been forgotten or used up. Well, we're going to talk about where they come from and why we need to be very, very careful with them to keep our home clean and safe.

What Are Old Gadgets? (Or E-Waste!)

Imagine your favorite toy car. What happens when it breaks and you can't play with it anymore? Maybe a wheel falls off, or the remote control stops working. You might put it aside, and then maybe it gets thrown away. Well, "e-waste" is just like that, but it's for things that use electricity or batteries! The 'e' stands for electronic. It means old cell phones that don't make calls, broken TV screens that are dark, computers that won't turn on, and even old video game consoles that have stopped working. These are all things we plug into the wall or use batteries for to make them work. When they stop working or we don't want them anymore because we have new ones, they become "e-waste." It's like electronic rubbish! And guess what? There's a lot of it! Think about how many people have a phone or a computer. When those break, they become e-waste. It's not just big things, even tiny earphones or old chargers are e-waste. Did you know? Even tiny headphones can become e-waste when they break or you get new ones! Every little bit adds up to a big pile!

Where Do All These Old Gadgets Come From?

This is a bit of a tricky part, my friends. Many, many of these old gadgets come from very far away. They come from countries that are very rich. In those countries, people buy new phones, new TVs, and new computers all the time. Sometimes, they buy a new gadget even if their old one still works a little bit! When they get a brand-new phone, they might quickly throw away their old one. They don't want it anymore. And guess what? Sometimes, instead of staying in their own country, these old phones, TVs, and computers are put on big, big ships. These ships travel across the wide ocean, all the way to our country, Nigeria. When they arrive here, they come to our markets. Some of these gadgets might be fixed and sold again, which is good! But many of them are already very, very broken, or so old that they can't really be fixed well. It's like getting a big box of old, broken toys that other kids in a far-away land don't want anymore. Did you know? Some rich countries make so much e-waste every single year that they don't know what to do with it all! So, some of it ends up here.

Why Are Lots of Old Gadgets a Big Problem?

You might think, "Oh, old gadgets are fun! Maybe we can fix them and play with them!" And sometimes, we can fix a few things, which is clever! But having too many old gadgets that don't work or can't be fixed is a really big problem for our home, Nigeria, and for our wonderful Mother Earth. First, they take up a lot of space. Imagine a huge mountain made of broken TVs, old phones, and computer parts! That's a lot of space that could be used for growing yummy food on farms, or for beautiful parks where children can play and animals can live. We don't want our land to be covered in rubbish. Second, and this is super, super important: many old gadgets have special tiny parts inside them. These tiny parts have special chemicals. Think of them like hidden bad stuff! These chemicals can be very, very bad for us and for nature. If these old gadgets are just left outside in big piles in the sun, in the rain, or near our rivers, these bad chemicals can leak out. They can go into our soil, which makes the soil sick and hard for plants to grow. They can go into the water we drink every day, making our water dirty and unsafe. This dirty water and soil can make people and animals sick. They can cause tummy aches, make our skin itch, or even make us feel unwell in other ways. It can hurt the plants and trees too. We need clean water and healthy soil to live and grow! Did you know? One tiny phone battery, if it's thrown away wrongly, can pollute a lot of water – enough water to fill many bathtubs! That's how powerful these chemicals can be.

What Happens to the Broken Stuff in Our Markets?

When these old gadgets come to Nigeria, they often end up in big, busy markets. You might see people called informal recyclers. They are often working hard, trying to take them apart. They might try to fix some of them to sell again. They might take parts from one broken TV to fix another, or try to get out valuable metals like copper. This trying to reuse things is a good idea because it helps use things again! But many gadgets are just too old or too broken to fix safely or properly. And sometimes, the people working with them don't know about the dangerous chemicals inside. They might not have special gloves or masks to keep them safe. This is not good for their health. Then, what happens to the parts that can't be used or fixed? Often, they are just dumped. They are thrown into big piles of rubbish, sometimes even burned to get out tiny bits of metal. Burning them makes very bad smoke that is dangerous to breathe. This is where the bad chemicals can start to leak out and hurt everything around. Did you know? There are special places called "proper recycling centers" where old gadgets can be taken apart safely by experts who wear special protective gear! We need more of those in Africa.

What Can We Do to Help Our Home?

We can all be superheroes for our home, Africa! Even us kids can help make a big difference. First, we can try to use our gadgets for a very, very long time. Don't throw something away just because you want the newest one. If something breaks, maybe we can try to fix it! Ask an adult if your broken toy or old tablet can be repaired by someone in the community. This saves money and saves Mother Earth from getting more rubbish. Second, if something is truly, truly broken and cannot be fixed at all, we should try to recycle it properly. Recycling means taking the good parts from old things to make brand-new things. It's like taking the good wheels from your old toy car to make a new one, so nothing is wasted! We need more places in Nigeria where we can take old electronics to be recycled safely, not just dumped or burned. We can also talk to our parents, teachers, and friends about this important problem. When we learn together, we can find better ways to deal with old electronics. Maybe our communities can have special collection days for e-waste, so it goes to a safe place. Did you know? When you recycle an old aluminum can, it can be back on the shelf as a new can in just 60 days! Imagine if we could do that for more parts of our old gadgets!

Let's Protect Our Beautiful Africa!

Our home, Africa, is a beautiful place with amazing animals, green plants, and wonderful people. We don't want it to be covered in mountains of broken gadgets that make our land sick. We want our air to be clean for us to breathe, our water to be clean for us to drink, and our soil to be healthy for growing yummy food. By understanding what e-waste is and why it's a problem, we can all help. Let's choose to fix things when we can, reuse things instead of buying new, and recycle properly when things are really broken. Let's tell our friends and family about it too, so everyone can join in. Together, we can make sure our Nigeria stays clean and healthy for all of us, for the animals, and for the plants too! Thank you for listening, my wonderful friends! See you next time for more important news from our amazing Africa!
summary: Old electronic gadgets from rich countries are coming to Nigeria, creating a big pile of dangerous e-waste that can harm our land, water, and people, but we can help by fixing, reusing, and recycling.
whyMatters: This is important because too many old, broken gadgets can make our home, Nigeria, dirty and unhealthy, hurting our land, water, and even us, so we need to learn how to deal with them safely.

originalTitle: Zelensky visits Saudi Arabia after offering Ukraine's drone expertise
title: President Zelensky's Big Trip to Make New Friends!
content:
Hello from Your African News Reporter!

Hi everyone! It's your friendly news reporter here, bringing you super exciting news from around our big, beautiful world! Guess what? Even though we are here in Africa, there are important things happening far, far away that teach us about friendship and helping each other. Let's find out!

Who is Mr. Zelensky?

Today, we are going to talk about a very important person named Mr. Zelensky. He is the President of a country called Ukraine. Being a President means he is like the leader of his whole country. He helps make big decisions for everyone who lives there, just like your school principal helps your school.

Where is Ukraine?

Ukraine is a country far, far away from us, across the big oceans. It's a place with lovely green fields and big, tall mountains. Right now, Ukraine is going through a tough time, so Mr. Zelensky works very hard to keep his people safe and strong. He wants to find good friends for Ukraine.

And Where is Saudi Arabia?

Now, imagine another country called Saudi Arabia. This is also very far away! Saudi Arabia is known for its really hot deserts and amazing, tall buildings. It's a land with a rich history and lots of interesting cultures. Mr. Zelensky went all the way to visit this country!

What Are Drones?

So, why did Mr. Zelensky travel so far? He went to talk about something super cool: drones! Have you ever seen a toy drone fly in the sky? Drones are like flying robots. They can be very small, like a bird, or very big, like a small airplane. Drones are amazing because they can fly without a pilot sitting inside them. Someone on the ground controls them with a special remote. They often have cameras attached, so they can take pictures and videos from high up in the sky. Imagine seeing your whole town from above! Drones can do many helpful things. Farmers use them to check their crops. Scientists use them to study animals in faraway places. Sometimes, they can even deliver packages right to your door! They are like little helpers in the sky. Did you know? The very first drones were invented a long, long time ago, even before your parents were born! But they have become much smarter and more useful today.

Ukraine's Special Drone Skills

Now, Ukraine has become very, very good at using drones. Because they are trying to keep their country safe, their people have learned a lot about how drones can help. They know how to make them fly very well and use them for many different tasks. They have gained lots of 'expertise.' Expertise means being really, really good at something because you have done it a lot. Think of your best friend who is super good at skipping or drawing – that's their expertise! Ukraine has special knowledge about drones. They know how to fix them, how to make them better, and how to use them safely and cleverly. This knowledge is like a special treasure that they can share. Did you know? Some drones can even fly by themselves using special computer brains, without someone controlling them all the time!

The Big Visit and Sharing Ideas

Mr. Zelensky went to Saudi Arabia to share this special knowledge. He wanted to talk to the leaders there about how Ukraine uses drones. It's like when you learn a new game and you teach your friends how to play it too. He offered Ukraine's 'drone expertise.' This means he offered to teach Saudi Arabia all about drones. He wants to help other countries learn how to use these flying robots in smart ways. It's a way of making friends and working together.

Why is This Important NOW?

Why is sharing this knowledge important right now? Well, countries always want to be safe and strong. Learning about new technology like drones can help countries protect their people and their land. It helps them be prepared for anything. Sometimes, countries need to make new friends and help each other. When one country is good at something, like Ukraine is with drones, sharing that skill can make the whole world a little bit safer and smarter. It's like teamwork, but for countries! There is a feeling of 'urgency' for Ukraine to make these friendships and share ideas. Urgency means something needs to happen quickly and is very important right now. Ukraine wants to make sure everyone is safe and prepared, and making friends helps a lot. Did you know? When countries work together and share ideas, it's called 'diplomacy.' It helps avoid arguments and makes the world a better place for everyone, including us here in Africa!

Friendship Across the World

So, Mr. Zelensky's trip was all about making new friends and sharing special skills. He wants to show that even when things are tough, countries can still help each other. This is a very important lesson for all of us. Just like how you help your friends at school, countries help other countries. Ukraine is sharing its knowledge, and Saudi Arabia might become a good friend who can also help Ukraine in other ways. It's a two-way street! This kind of friendship is important for everyone. When countries learn from each other, they become stronger and wiser. This can help bring peace and safety to many parts of the world. And that's good news for all of us!

That's All For Now!

So, there you have it! A big trip, flying robots, and lots of friendship. Isn't it wonderful how people from different places can come together to help each other? Stay tuned for more amazing news from your African reporter! Bye for now!
summary: President Zelensky from Ukraine visited Saudi Arabia to share special knowledge about flying robots called drones, hoping to make new friends and keep countries safe.
whyMatters: This is important because when countries share their special skills and make friends, they can help each other stay safe and strong, making the world a better place for everyone.

originalTitle: Pain Hallgrimsson's overriding emotion after Czechs end Irish hopes
title: It's Okay to Feel Sad, Coach! Ireland's World Cup Dream
content:
Hello, Young Football Fans!

Hello everyone! It's your kid-friendly news reporter here, bringing you stories from our big, beautiful world. Today, we're going to talk about football! I know so many of you love playing football with your friends, running fast and kicking the ball. It's a game that brings so much joy and excitement, doesn't it? Football is wonderful because it makes us feel many things. Sometimes we feel super happy when our team wins. Sometimes we feel a little bit sad when our team loses. That's totally normal. Even grown-ups feel these things, like the coaches and players!

What is the World Cup?

Imagine the biggest football party in the whole world! That's what the World Cup is. It's a very special tournament where football teams from many, many different countries come together to play. Every team wants to win the shiny golden trophy. It's a huge honour to play in the World Cup. Teams work super hard for many years just to get a chance to be there. It's like the biggest dream for any football player. Did you know? The World Cup happens only once every four years! That makes it extra, extra special and exciting when it finally comes around!

A Team Called Ireland

There's a country far away called Ireland. They have a football team, just like many countries in Africa have amazing teams! This team, the Republic of Ireland, had a big dream. Their dream was to go and play in the next big World Cup. They worked very hard, practiced a lot, and played many games to try and make that dream come true. Their coach, the person who helps the team and tells them what to do, is called Mr. Heimir Hallgrimsson. Mr. Hallgrimsson is like the teacher for the football team. He helps them learn new moves and play together well. He really wanted his team to go to the World Cup.

The Big Game in Prague

To get to the World Cup, the Irish team had to play a very important game. They played against another strong team from a country called the Czech Republic. The game was played in a city called Prague. It was a super exciting game! Both teams played with all their might. They ran fast, kicked the ball hard, and tried their very best to score goals. The fans in the stadium cheered very loudly for their teams. After all the time was up, the score was tied! Nobody had won yet. What happens then?

What are Penalties?

When a football game is tied, and one team *has* to win to go to the next stage, they sometimes have to do something called a 'penalty shootout.' It's like a special test to see who is best. Here's how it works: Each team chooses five players. These players take turns kicking the ball from a special spot in front of the goal. Only the goalkeeper is there to try and stop the ball. It's very tricky for the player to kick the ball in, and it's very tricky for the goalkeeper to stop it. Everyone watching holds their breath! It's exciting, but it can also be very tense. Did you know? The goalkeeper's job during a penalty shootout is one of the toughest! They have to guess which way the ball will go in a tiny fraction of a second!

Sad News for Ireland

Sadly, during the penalty shootout, the Republic of Ireland team did not score enough goals. This meant that the other team, the Czech Republic, won the game. Because they lost, Ireland cannot go to the World Cup this time. This was very sad news for the players, for the fans, and especially for their coach, Mr. Heimir Hallgrimsson. They had all worked so hard and hoped so much.

Coach Heimir's Sadness

After the game, someone asked Mr. Hallgrimsson how he felt. He said he felt "pain." When he said "pain," he didn't mean his body hurt. He meant his heart felt very, very sad. It was a deep sadness because his team's big dream had ended. It's okay to feel this way. When we really, really want something, and it doesn't happen, it can make us feel very sad. This is true for everyone, even strong coaches like Mr. Hallgrimsson. Remember, it's brave to show your feelings. It shows how much you cared and how much you tried.

Learning from Losing

Even though it's sad to lose, playing sports teaches us important lessons. When a team loses, they don't just give up. They think about what happened. They learn from their mistakes. They go back to practice and try even harder. They might learn new ways to play, or practice their kicks more. Losing can make a team stronger for the next time. Did you know? Many famous football players have lost big games before, but they kept practicing and went on to win even bigger games later! Never give up!

Never Give Up!

So, the Republic of Ireland team will not be at the next World Cup. But that does not mean their football journey is over! They will keep playing, keep practicing, and keep trying. They will aim for the *next* World Cup. That's what being a good sport is all about: trying your best, being brave, and never giving up, even when things are tough. Just like you try your best in school, even when a subject is hard.

Cheering for Everyone

We should always cheer for teams that try their best, no matter if they win or lose. They showed great effort and spirit. Football is a game that teaches us about teamwork, hard work, and dealing with winning and losing. We love football in Africa because it brings us together. It makes us happy. And it teaches us to always keep trying. So let's clap for the Irish team for trying so hard, and wish them luck for next time! Did you know? Football is played in almost every country in the world, making it the most popular sport on Earth! How amazing is that?
summary: The Republic of Ireland football team's dream of playing in the World Cup ended after they lost a penalty shootout, making their coach, Mr. Heimir Hallgrimsson, feel very sad.
whyMatters: This story helps us understand that it's okay to feel sad when we lose, even for grown-ups and sports heroes, and that trying our best and learning from challenges is what really matters in sports and in life.
